title: Dr Tim's Preferred Cannula Technique For the Cheek
filename: Dr Tim_s Preferred Cannula Technique For the Cheek.en.vtt
excerpt: [Music] so getting your candor in all sorts of ways of getting in as in there's a this kind of stirring thing with a needle where you put the needle in and then go round and round my idea of what's going on there is you're the sharpest bit the needle is doing something underneath…

segments.text: [Music] so getting your candor in all sorts of ways of getting in as in there's a this kind of stirring thing with a needle where you put the needle in and then go round and round my idea of what's going on there is you're the sharpest bit the needle is doing something underneath and then the blunt bit that's not really that good at stretching the skin is just going around so it's not necessarily that effective and mostly I don't think you need to do very much at all other than just aim properly just pop a little and swab on top of that so anyone who's seen me you do this before I think I'm unusually gentle compared with many I really don't like the idea of tearing through to get some way I like to try and find an easy route so I'm just trying lots of different angles maintaining my overall direction of aim

and trying to sneak through without causing any trauma and we're right back now so now I'm right where I want to be I'm on the superior surface of the zygoma and this is where particularly where the cannula is very sculptural I'm just kind of it alot like shaping it you know if you're a sculptor and you're chipping away a bone but it's the reverse because additive I'm just adding little bits I'm seeing what the result is like I'm checking it doing a bit more and just gradually increasing the volume
